summary: Matched two mnname functions, fn_80238540 and mnName_80239FFC, with header/type updates needed to express the call targets and GObj/JObj/Text layout. The PR reports clean ninja verification and 100.0 fuzzy match for all functions. Review specifically pushed the implementation away from raw pointer arithmetic toward using the actual jobj/GObj struct fields.
what_changed: Decompiled fn_80238540 as a dispatcher on mn_804A04F0.x10, calling mnName_MainInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, or mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput for cases 0, 1, and 2 respectively.

Decompiled mnName_80239FFC to retrieve a JObj from the menu-name GObj data, remove all children from its child JObj with HSD_JObjRemoveAll, and free/reset associated HSD_Text with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 when present.
Added includes in mnname.c for mnmain.h, mnnamenew.h, and baselib/gobj.h so the newly decompiled functions could use concrete declarations.
Introduced MnName_GObj in mnname.h with an embedded HSD_GObj, an x38 field, and an HSD_Text* text field.
Updated prototypes from UNK_PARAMS to typed HSD_GObj* signatures for mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, mnName_MainInput, fn_80238540, mnName_80239FFC, and mnNameNew_MainInput.
smart_moves: Converted dispatcher callees to typed HSD_GObj* prototypes across mnname.h and mnnamenew.h, avoiding mismatched placeholder signatures once fn_80238540 called them directly.
Modeled the menu-name object wrapper as MnName_GObj instead of leaving all accesses as byte offsets, making the text cleanup field explicit.
Handled the possible NULL JObj before accessing jobj->child, matching the safe control flow shown in the diff.
Set mn->text to NULL after freeing it, preserving the cleanup semantics visible in mnName_80239FFC.
Responded to review direction by avoiding raw pointer math in favor of actual struct usage where possible.
decomp_lessons: Small menu dispatcher functions can often be matched cleanly by typing the global state field used in the switch and by correcting prototypes for all branch targets.
When a GObj callback needs data beyond the base HSD_GObj, define a wrapper struct with the embedded HSD_GObj at offset 0 rather than repeatedly casting to u8* and using offsets.
Reviewers prefer concrete HSD struct field access, such as jobj->child, over pointer arithmetic, even when pointer arithmetic may produce a match.
Cleanup functions should preserve both resource-freeing behavior and post-free state updates, such as clearing HSD_Text pointers.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_PARAMS with typed HSD_GObj* signatures can unlock direct calls and improve future decomp readability.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing address-based names for still-unknown functions, such as fn_80238540 and mnName_80239FFC.
Used subsystem prefixes consistently: mnName_* for existing name menu functions and mnNameNew_* for new-name-entry functions.
Named the wrapper struct MnName_GObj to indicate it represents mnname-specific data layered on top of HSD_GObj.
Kept unknown struct member naming as x38 where the field purpose was not evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a switch on (s32) mn_804A04F0.x10 to reproduce the input-state dispatcher for fn_80238540.
Matched mnName_80239FFC by casting HSD_GObj* to a mnname-specific wrapper struct and using HSD_JObj/HSD_Text APIs rather than opaque placeholders.
Added exact baselib forward/type includes needed to make typed prototypes and field accesses compile.
Verified by reported clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for the decompiled functions.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: This should use the actual `jobj` struct, not pointer math.
review_feedback.lesson: Prefer actual HSD_JObj/HSD_GObj struct fields over byte-offset casts when the struct layout is known.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine MnName_GObj fields, especially x38 or the source of the JObj pointer.
Search for other mnname functions still using UNK_PARAMS that can be typed as HSD_GObj* or HSD_JObj* callbacks.
Look for similar menu cleanup functions using HSD_JObjRemoveAll and HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 to standardize resource teardown patterns.
Audit remaining raw pointer arithmetic around HSD_GObj user data in menu code and replace with known struct fields where supported.
